source: Invested by Aleph — Semiconductors, China/Taiwan fabs, global warming (China's Not Getting Taiwan's Fabs)
summary: Scaled foundation model companies are dramatically better investments post-reasoning models due to emergence of data flywheel
summary_extended: Baker argues that the emergence of reasoning models (via RLVF and test-time compute) has fundamentally changed the investment case for scaled foundation model companies by creating a user-data-product flywheel that did not previously exist in standard LLMs. Specifically, he contends that a large user base now directly feeds back into model quality improvement, giving companies with massive user bases a compounding structural advantage that smaller or newer entrants cannot easily replicate. Baker identifies OpenAI, Anthropic, XAI, and Google DeepMind as the primary beneficiaries of this dynamic, viewing them as a durable oligopoly that can now be valued on revenue multiples rather than speculative metrics. The investment implication is that scaled foundation model companies are dramatically better investments post-reasoning than they were before, and that this structural moat makes them long-term holds. Notably, most of the named beneficiaries (OpenAI, Anthropic, XAI) are private, limiting direct public equity expression of this thesis.
detail: Reasoning models (RLVF, test-time compute) created a user-data-product flywheel that previously did not exist in LLMs. Now having a large user base feeds back into product quality, making OpenAI, Anthropic, XAI, and Google DeepMind structurally advantaged. Baker views these as now-valueable on revenue multiples and likely to form a durable oligopoly.
confidence_evidence: Baker explicitly states 'these scaled foundation model companies are much better investments post-reasoning' and elaborates the structural mechanism at length
quote_fragment: scaled foundation model companies are much better investments post-reasoning

ticker_audit.reasoning: Google DeepMind is explicitly named by Baker as one of the four scaled foundation model companies forming the durable oligopoly, and GOOGL is the publicly listed vehicle for that exposure.

META was implied but Baker's named oligopoly is OpenAI, Anthropic, XAI, and Google DeepMind — Meta's Llama is open-source and does not benefit from the closed user-data-product flywheel mechanism Baker describes. META is not a direct subject or beneficiary of this specific thesis.

MSFT is the primary public-market vehicle for OpenAI exposure through its deep partnership and equity stake, and benefits directly from OpenAI's flywheel advantage as the exclusive cloud and product distribution partner, making it a legitimate direct beneficiary of this thesis.
